arguments.task: Visit the official Linear developer documentation and other official Linear pages needed to verify: one-line API/app description, authentication methods, whether API access is self-serve or gated, API surface type (REST/GraphQL/Both/None), breadth of API coverage, rate limit notes, and whether Linear provides an official MCP server or documentation. Prefer first-party sources such as developers.linear.app, linear.app pricing/access docs, changelog/docs, GitHub official org pages, and any official MCP registry pages. Return the exact supporting URLs and concise extracted findings only from those pages.

raw_result.description: Linear provides a public GraphQL API and SDKs for building apps and integrations for Linear.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official developer docs mention OAuth 2.0 authentication and personal API keys. Verified header examples include `Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>` for OAuth and `Authorization: <KEY>` for personal API keys; personal API key classification is marked as token-based.
raw_result.gating_reason: Developer documentation is publicly accessible and the gathered evidence shows standard OAuth 2.0 and personal API key auth with no verified contact-sales or approval gate for initial API use.

api_surface.breadth: Public API is GraphQL and described as the same API Linear uses internally. Gathered schema evidence indicates broad coverage with roughly 161 queries, 370 mutations, 79 subscriptions, and 567 object types, covering core work-management entities such as issues, projects, cycles, teams, and documents.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP server or official MCP documentation was verified from the gathered evidence. This is treated as unverified absence, so human review is recommended.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Official rate limiting page states Linear uses a leaky bucket algorithm. Gathered evidence indicates hourly request limits of 2,500/hour for API keys and 5,000/hour for OAuth apps, plus complexity-based limits including a 3,000,000/hour complexity limit for API keys and a 10,000 maximum complexity per query. Rate-limit headers are documented, including request and complexity limit/remaining/reset headers.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer hub exists; public GraphQL docs are available; Apollo-hosted public schema reference is exposed; docs state the public API is the same one used internally; documented OAuth and personal API key auth support straightforward agent integration.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Self-serve access was inferred from the public docs and standard auth setup but not directly confirmed on a pricing/access page in the gathered evidence. MCP existence was not verified from an official source; only lack of evidence was observed.

Rate-limit headers are documented, including request and complexity limit/remaining/reset headers.
